decisions.title: Phoenix Project V1 Main Goal: Working MVP
decisions.detail: The primary objective for Version 1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a fully functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP).

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: V1 MVP Deadline: September 1st
decisions.detail: The working MVP for Version 1 must be completed and delivered by September 1st.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: Hard Deadline Due to Marketing Campaign
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline is a hard constraint because it is directly tied to the marketing campaign launch.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Marketing Campaign Launch Date: September 5th
decisions.detail: The marketing campaign for the Phoenix Project is scheduled to launch on September 5th.

evidence.quote: marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: Ads Already Booked
decisions.detail: Advertisements for the project have already been booked, underscoring the importance of the launch schedule.

evidence.quote: Yes, we've already booked the ads.

decisions.title: V1 Must Include User Authentication
decisions.detail: User authentication is a mandatory feature for the initial Version 1 release.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: V1 Must Include a Working Project Dashboard
decisions.detail: A functional project dashboard is a mandatory feature for the initial Version 1 release.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: System Performance Must Be 'Super Fast'
decisions.detail: The system is required to exhibit high performance, described as 'super fast'.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend Technology: React
decisions.detail: React has been selected as the technology for the frontend development.

evidence.quote: For the stack, I've decided: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Backend Technology: Python (FastAPI)
decisions.detail: Python with FastAPI has been selected as the technology for the backend service.

evidence.quote: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Python Chosen Due to Existing Libraries and Team Expertise
decisions.detail: Python was chosen for the backend because the team already has existing libraries built in Python, leveraging current team skills and resources.

evidence.quote: I think it makes the most sense for our team since we already have libraries built in Python.

decisions.title: Database Technology Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The choice of database technology was initially an open question.

evidence.quote: What about the database?

decisions.title: Database Will Be PostgreSQL
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL has been chosen as the database system for the project.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL. Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Standard Relational Model for Database
decisions.detail: A standard relational database model will be used with PostgreSQL.

evidence.quote: Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Session Management Technology Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The specific technology or method for session management was an open question.

evidence.quote: what about session management? Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Redis Deferred to V2
decisions.detail: The implementation of Redis for session management will be postponed to Version 2, unless performance issues necessitate its earlier adoption.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: Standard PostgreSQL Sessions for V1
decisions.detail: For Version 1, standard PostgreSQL database sessions will be used for session management.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: Simplicity is a Guiding Principle for V1
decisions.detail: The project emphasizes simplicity in design and implementation for Version 1.

evidence.quote: Keep it simple.

decisions.title: Budget Saving as a Factor for Technology Choices
decisions.detail: Decisions regarding technology and implementation should consider budget implications.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: User Authentication Implementation Method Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The method for implementing user authentication was an open question.

evidence.quote: How are we doing user auth?

decisions.title: Third-Party Authentication Solution Preferred
decisions.detail: The preference is to utilize a third-party service for user authentication rather than building a custom solution.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: Integrate Firebase Authentication for V1
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be integrated for user authentication in Version 1.

evidence.quote: for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Chosen for Quick Integration
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication was selected partly because it allows for quick integration.

evidence.quote: It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Must Handle Social Logins Easily
decisions.detail: The chosen authentication solution (Firebase Auth) must easily facilitate social logins.

evidence.quote: It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Must Match Pricing Requirements
decisions.detail: The selected authentication solution must align with the project's pricing requirements.

evidence.quote: It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Must Support Google Sign-in
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow users to sign in using their Google accounts.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: Firebase Auth Must Support Email Logins
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be configured to allow users to sign in using their email addresses.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: User Registration Acceptance Criteria Needed (Resolved)
decisions.detail: Acceptance criteria for user registration flow were initially an open question.

evidence.quote: Do we have acceptance criteria for user registration?

decisions.title: Required Fields for User Registration Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The specific fields required for user registration were initially an open question.

evidence.quote: What fields do we collect?

decisions.title: User Registration Must Collect Email
decisions.detail: During user signup, the system must collect the user's email address.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: User Registration Must Collect Full Name
decisions.detail: During user signup, the system must collect the user's full name.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: User Registration Must Collect Role
decisions.detail: During user signup, the system must collect the user's role.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role.

decisions.title: Address and Telephone Number Not Required for V1 Registration
decisions.detail: For Version 1, user registration will not require collecting address or telephone number.

evidence.quote: We don't need address or telephone number for now.

decisions.title: User Registration Must Collect Company Name
decisions.detail: Due to the B2B nature of the project, the user registration process must include collecting the company name.

evidence.quote: Wait, we need to collect company name too! We are B2B.

decisions.title: Project is B2B Focused
decisions.detail: The Phoenix Project is aimed at a Business-to-Business (B2B) audience.

evidence.quote: We are B2B.

decisions.title: Deployment Strategy Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The deployment strategy for both frontend and backend applications was an open question.

evidence.quote: What about deployment?

decisions.title: Cloud Run Proposed for FastAPI App Deployment (Confirmed with Security Constraint)
decisions.detail: Cloud Run was proposed for deploying the FastAPI backend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app and Vercel for the React app.

decisions.title: Vercel Proposed for React App Deployment (Confirmed)
decisions.detail: Vercel was proposed and accepted for deploying the React frontend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app and Vercel for the React app.

decisions.title: Vercel Accepted for Frontend Deployment
decisions.detail: Vercel has been confirmed as the deployment platform for the React frontend.

evidence.quote: Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Security of Cloud Run for Backend is a Concern (Resolved)
decisions.detail: A concern was raised regarding the security of using Cloud Run for backend deployment.

evidence.quote: but for the backend, is Cloud Run secure?

decisions.title: Database Must Be in a Private VPC
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL database instance must be deployed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) for enhanced security.

evidence.quote: Yes, we will place the database in a private VPC and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: Cloud Run Connects to DB via Serverless VPC Access
decisions.detail: Cloud Run will connect to the private VPC containing the database using Serverless VPC Access.

evidence.quote: connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access.

decisions.title: PostgreSQL Instance Not Exposed to Public Internet
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL database instance must not be accessible from the public internet, ensuring a secure posture.

evidence.quote: That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.

decisions.title: Security is a Strict Technical Constraint
decisions.detail: Security requirements are considered a strict technical constraint for the project.

evidence.quote: This is a strict technical constraint for security.

decisions.title: Authentication Flow Needs Thorough Testing
decisions.detail: The user authentication flow requires comprehensive testing to ensure its robustness and correctness.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.

decisions.title: Session Duration (Token Expiration) Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The duration for user sessions (token expiration) was an open question.

evidence.quote: If a user logs in, how long does the session last?

decisions.title: Token Expiration Set to 24 Hours
decisions.detail: User session tokens will be configured to expire after 24 hours.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

decisions.title: Token Expiration is Adjustable
decisions.detail: The 24-hour token expiration setting can be modified at a later stage if needed.

evidence.quote: We can adjust it later.

decisions.title: Dashboard Metrics Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The specific metrics to be displayed on the project dashboard were an open question.

evidence.quote: What metrics are we displaying?

decisions.title: Dashboard Must Display Active User Metrics
decisions.detail: The project dashboard is required to show metrics related to active users.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Dashboard Must Display System Status
decisions.detail: The project dashboard is required to show the current system status.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Dashboard Must Display Project Lists
decisions.detail: The project dashboard is required to display lists of projects.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Detailing Dashboard Features Deferred to Next Sync
decisions.detail: Further detailed discussions and specifications for dashboard features will be covered in the next sync meeting.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.title: Firebase Provisioning Responsibility Open Question (Resolved)
decisions.detail: The individual responsible for Firebase provisioning was an open question.

evidence.quote: One open question: who is handling the Firebase provisioning?

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Needed by Next Week
decisions.detail: The Firebase configuration keys are required by the end of the next week.

evidence.quote: I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Mike Assigned Firebase Provisioning
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase Config Keys Ready by July 8th
decisions.detail: The Firebase configuration keys are expected to be ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Sync with Marketing Team on Dashboard Design Next Wednesday
decisions.detail: A synchronization meeting with the marketing team to discuss dashboard design is scheduled for next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Implement user authentication system integrating Firebase Authentication.
Configure Firebase Authentication for Google sign-in.
Configure Firebase Authentication for email logins.
Implement user registration flow to collect user's email, full name, role, and company name. Do not collect address or telephone number for V1.
Develop a functional project dashboard to display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Design and implement PostgreSQL database schema for user data and project data.
Implement session management using standard PostgreSQL database sessions.
Thoroughly test the user authentication flow.

constraints.text: Working MVP must be delivered by September 1st. This is a hard deadline tied to the marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
Backend technology must be Python due to existing libraries and team expertise.
Database must use a standard relational model with PostgreSQL.
Simplicity is a guiding principle for V1 design and implementation.
Chosen authentication solution (Firebase Auth) must allow for quick integration and easily handle social logins.
The PostgreSQL database instance must be deployed within a private VPC and connected via Serverless VPC Access. It must not be exposed to the public internet.
Security requirements are a strict technical constraint.
Firebase configuration keys are required by July 8th.

architectural_decisions.text: Frontend will be developed using React.
Backend service will be developed using Python with FastAPI.
PostgreSQL will be used as the database system.
Session management for V1 will use standard PostgreSQL database sessions; Redis is deferred to V2 unless performance issues arise.
User authentication will be implemented by integrating Firebase Authentication.
Frontend (React) will be deployed on Vercel.
Backend (FastAPI) will be deployed on Cloud Run.
User session tokens will be configured to expire after 24 hours, with the setting being adjustable.

acceptance_criteria: Given the system is deployed, When a new user attempts to register with Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name, Then the registration shall be successful, and the user shall be authenticated.
Given an unauthenticated user, When they attempt to sign in using their Google account, Then they shall be successfully authenticated and granted access.
Given an unauthenticated user, When they attempt to sign in using their registered email and password, Then they shall be successfully authenticated and granted access.
Given an authenticated user, When their session token has reached 24 hours since issuance, Then their session shall expire, requiring re-authentication.
Given an authenticated user, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display metrics related to active users.
Given an authenticated user, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display the current system status.
Given an authenticated user, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard shall display lists of projects.
Given the PostgreSQL database is deployed, When attempting to access the database from the public internet, Then all connection attempts shall be rejected.
Given the Cloud Run service for the backend, When it attempts to connect to the PostgreSQL database, Then the connection shall be established exclusively via Serverless VPC Access.
Given any critical user action (e.g., login, dashboard load), When the action is performed, Then the system response time must be 'super fast' (per D008).
test_scenarios: Verify a user can successfully register by providing a unique email, full name, role, and company name.
Verify a user can successfully log in using their registered Google account credentials.
Verify a user can successfully log in using their registered email and password.
Verify an authenticated user can access the Project Dashboard and view its contents.
Verify the Project Dashboard displays initial or placeholder data for active user metrics, system status, and project lists upon first access.
Verify that attempting to register with an already existing email address results in an appropriate error message and prevents account creation.
Verify that attempting to log in with incorrect email/password or Google credentials results in an appropriate error message and prevents access.
Verify a user is automatically logged out if their session remains inactive for more than 24 hours.
Verify the system prevents registration attempts that include address or telephone number fields.
Verify that direct external access to the PostgreSQL database from public IP addresses is blocked.
edge_cases: Attempt user registration with valid but extremely long values for Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields.
Test concurrent login attempts from the same user account across multiple devices or browser sessions.
Assess system responsiveness and performance during peak load conditions for authentication and dashboard data retrieval.
Verify system behavior when a user attempts to access authenticated routes immediately after their session token has expired.
Test the authentication flow for potential race conditions or unusual timing of requests (e.g., rapid login/logout).
Evaluate the impact on authentication and dashboard loading if the Firebase Authentication service experiences a momentary outage.
Test the behavior when a registered user attempts to sign up again with the same email via Google Sign-in and vice-versa.
unresolved_behaviors: Quantification of "super fast" performance for key system interactions and data loading (e.g., acceptable response times in milliseconds). (Ownership: PM/QA)
Specific definitions and expected data formats for "active user metrics," "system status," and "project lists" to be displayed on the dashboard. (Ownership: PM/QA)
Detailed UI/UX specifications for the user registration and login flows, including password requirements, error message presentation, and 'forgot password' functionality. (Ownership: PM/QA)
Clarification on which specific social login providers (beyond Google) are intended to be supported for V1, given D024 mentions 'social logins easily.' (Ownership: PM/QA)

decision_history: The primary objective for Version 1 (V1) of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a fully functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st. This is a hard deadline tied directly to a marketing campaign launching on September 5th, for which ads have already been booked.
V1 must include user authentication and a working project dashboard, with the system required to be 'super fast'. Simplicity and budget saving are guiding principles for V1 design and implementation.

Technology stack decisions: React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend, leveraging existing Python libraries and team expertise. PostgreSQL with a standard relational model will be used for the database.
Session management for V1 will use standard PostgreSQL sessions to save budget, with Redis deferred to V2 unless performance issues necessitate earlier adoption.

Firebase Authentication will be integrated for user authentication in V1, chosen for quick integration, easy social logins, and to match pricing requirements, supporting Google Sign-in and email logins.
User registration for this B2B project must collect email, full name, role, and company name. Address and telephone number are not required for V1.

Deployment strategy: Vercel accepted for React frontend deployment. Cloud Run proposed for FastAPI backend, with a security constraint that the PostgreSQL database instance must be in a private VPC, connected via Serverless VPC Access, and not exposed to the public internet.
User session tokens will be configured to expire after 24 hours, with the understanding that this setting is adjustable later.
The project dashboard in V1 must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists. Further detailing of dashboard features is deferred to the next sync meeting.
Mike has been assigned responsibility for Firebase provisioning and is expected to have the configuration keys ready by July 8th.
dependencies: Marketing campaign launch on 2026-09-05 is a hard dependency for the MVP, with ads already booked (External, High Impact).
Firebase configuration keys from Mike (Internal, High Impact) are needed by next week, specifically by July 8th, to enable Firebase integration.
Synchronization meeting with the Marketing Team on dashboard design (Internal, Cross-team) is scheduled for next Wednesday.
risks: **Deadline Miss (High Impact, Medium Likelihood):** The September 1st MVP deadline is a hard constraint due to the marketing campaign launch on September 5th and pre-booked ads. Missing this deadline would have significant business impact.

**Performance Issues (Medium Impact, Medium Likelihood):** The decision to defer Redis for session management to V2 and rely on standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 carries a risk of not meeting the 'super fast' performance requirement, especially given active user metrics will be displayed on the dashboard. This could impact user experience and the initial impression of the MVP.

**Authentication Flow Robustness (Medium Impact, Medium Likelihood):** The requirement for thorough testing of the authentication flow implies a risk of security vulnerabilities or functional defects if testing is not comprehensive enough.

**Dashboard Feature Scope Creep/Misalignment (Medium Impact, Medium Likelihood):** Detailing dashboard features is deferred to the next sync, which could lead to misalignment between development and stakeholder expectations if specific requirements are not captured and agreed upon in a timely manner.
alignment_gaps: Specific details and acceptance criteria for dashboard features (active user metrics, system status, project lists) are deferred, leading to potential misalignment on the exact scope and implementation until the next sync meeting.

The general requirement for 'super fast' system performance has not been quantified, which could lead to differing interpretations of success criteria by various stakeholders (e.g., engineering vs. product).
open_questions: What are the detailed specifications and acceptance criteria for the project dashboard features (active user metrics, system status, project lists) for V1? (PM/QA owned)
What are the specific, measurable performance criteria for 'super fast' system performance (e.g., response times, concurrency limits) that standard PostgreSQL sessions must meet for V1? (PM/QA owned)
What are the specific pricing requirements that Firebase Authentication must align with? (PM/Product owned)
Are there any other specific social login providers (besides Google) that Firebase Authentication must support for V1? (PM/Product owned)
What are the specific acceptance criteria and a detailed test plan for the thorough testing of the user authentication flow? (PM/QA owned)
milestones: Phoenix Project V1 MVP to be completed and delivered by 2026-09-01.
Marketing campaign for Phoenix Project to launch on 2026-09-05.
Firebase configuration keys (from Mike) to be ready by 2026-07-08.
Sync meeting with the marketing team on dashboard design to occur next Wednesday.

system_requirements: The Phoenix Project V1 SHALL deliver a fully functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP).
The system SHALL include user authentication as a mandatory feature for Version 1.
The system SHALL include a working project dashboard as a mandatory feature for Version 1.
The system SHALL exhibit high performance, defined as 'super fast' (non-functional).
The system SHALL utilize a third-party service for user authentication.
The chosen authentication solution SHALL easily facilitate social logins.
The chosen authentication solution SHALL support Google Sign-in.
The chosen authentication solution SHALL support email logins.
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's email address.
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's full name.
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's role.
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's company name.
The user registration process SHALL NOT require collecting the user's address or telephone number for Version 1.
The project dashboard SHALL display metrics related to active users.
The project dashboard SHALL display the current system status.
The project dashboard SHALL display lists of projects.
The user authentication flow SHALL undergo comprehensive testing to ensure robustness and correctness (non-functional).
The system SHALL prioritize simplicity in its design and implementation for Version 1 (non-functional).
The chosen authentication solution SHALL align with the project's pricing requirements (non-functional).
The system design and functionality SHALL cater to a Business-to-Business (B2B) audience (inferred from D035 - needs confirmation for specific impact).
data_flows: Frontend (React) to Backend (FastAPI) for API calls (inferred).
Frontend (React) to Firebase Authentication for user authentication and authorization flows (e.g., login, signup).
Firebase Authentication to Backend (FastAPI) for validating user tokens and potentially providing user identity data (e.g., email, full name, role, company name).
Backend (FastAPI) to PostgreSQL database for data persistence and standard session management.
Cloud Run (FastAPI Backend) to PostgreSQL database instance via Serverless VPC Access for secure communication.
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication API: Expected to handle user signup (email, full name, role, company name), email/password login, and Google social login. Output includes authentication token and user ID.
Backend API (FastAPI) Authentication Endpoints: Expected to receive Firebase authentication tokens, validate them, and establish internal user sessions. (inferred)
Backend API (FastAPI) Dashboard Endpoints: Expected to provide data for active user metrics, system status, and project lists. (Payloads to be defined)

constraints.text: The Phoenix Project Version 1 MVP must be completed and delivered by September 1st.
The September 1st deadline is a hard constraint due to its direct tie to the marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
Security requirements are a strict technical constraint for the project.
The PostgreSQL database instance must be deployed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
The PostgreSQL database instance must not be exposed to the public internet.
The backend development must leverage existing Python libraries and team expertise.
Decisions regarding technology and implementation should consider budget implications.
The chosen authentication solution (Firebase Auth) must allow for quick integration.
Firebase configuration keys are required by July 8th.

architectural_decisions.text: Database model: Standard relational model for PostgreSQL.
Session management for V1: Standard PostgreSQL database sessions.
Redis for session management is deferred to Version 2, unless performance issues necessitate earlier adoption.
User authentication solution: Integrate Firebase Authentication for Version 1.
User session token expiration: Configured to 24 hours, with the option to adjust later.
FastAPI backend deployment platform: Cloud Run.
Cloud Run connectivity to PostgreSQL database: Via Serverless VPC Access.

open_analysis_questions: What specific, measurable performance targets define 'super fast' system performance (e.g., response times, throughput, concurrent users)?
What specific components/aspects constitute 'System Status' to be displayed on the dashboard, and how will their status be determined (e.g., API health, database connection, external service health)?

What is the precise definition of 'active user' and what specific metrics (e.g., Daily Active Users, Monthly Active Users, concurrent users, average session duration) should be displayed on the dashboard?

What specific data points and functionalities are required for 'Project Lists' on the dashboard (e.g., project name, ID, status, owner, creation date, associated users, access controls, filtering/sorting capabilities)?

stakeholder.business_summary: The team has confirmed the Phoenix Project's core requirements and timeline for a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st. This deadline is critical as it aligns with a major marketing campaign launching on September 5th, for which ads are already booked. Key features like user authentication and a functional project dashboard are prioritized for the initial release.
key_impact: Meeting the September 1st MVP deadline is crucial to avoid disruption to the planned marketing campaign and booked advertisements, protecting marketing investment.
The product will provide essential user access and a functional dashboard for project tracking, improving user experience and engagement.
Development is focused on efficiency and budget by using existing team expertise and integrating a third-party solution (Firebase) for user logins, rather than building from scratch.
Robust security measures, including isolating the database from public access, are being implemented to protect user and company data, which is a strict requirement for the project.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the final, specific details and design elements required for the project dashboard, including the exact metrics and layout, to ensure it meets business needs?
How will 'super fast' system performance be objectively defined, measured, and confirmed to meet user expectations before launch?

issues.description: The PM View adds the year '2026' to several dates (e.g., '2026-09-05', '2026-07-08') when the original extracted decisions (D004, D057) only specify the month and day or 'next week/Wednesday'.
issues.suggestion: While '2026' is a reasonable inference for current context, it's technically an unconfirmed detail. It should either be omitted or explicitly stated as an assumed current year to maintain strict adherence to source evidence.

review.reviewer_notes: The report is well-structured and comprehensive. All significant claims in the Engineer, QA, PM, and Stakeholder views are traceable to the extracted decisions, with no critical hallucinations or misrepresentations. The quality of outputs is high, providing actionable information for each audience. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected. A minor info-level issue was noted regarding the PM view's date formatting, which assumes the year '2026' where it wasn't explicitly stated in the source. This does not block publishing.

source_map.decision_title: Database Must Be in a Private VPC

source_map.decision_title: PostgreSQL Instance Not Exposed to Public Internet
evidence_quotes: That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet.

source_map.decision_title: Security is a Strict Technical Constraint
evidence_quotes: This is a strict technical constraint for security.
We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.
If a user logs in, how long does the session last?
Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

evidence_quotes: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
